messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following cookie consent banner text for GDPR compliance:

"We use cookies to enhance your experience. By continuing to use this site, you agree to our use of cookies."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [FAIL] Invalid consent. "By continuing to use this site" is not a freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous indication of agreement under Art. 7. Pre-ticked or implied consent does not satisfy GDPR.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PASS] Not applicable on its face, though behavioural tracking cookies can infer health or other special category data — this risk is not addressed.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [FAIL] Vague. "Enhance your experience" is not a specific purpose. The types of cookies (analytics, marketing, functional) and their individual purposes are not disclosed.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [FAIL] Cannot be assessed. Without specifying which cookies are used and what data they collect, minimization cannot be evaluated.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [FAIL] Missing. No cookie duration or data retention period is stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [FAIL] Missing. No mechanism to withdraw consent, access data, or object to processing is provided. The right to withdraw consent must be as easy as giving it (Art. 7(3)).

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [FAIL] Missing. No mention of third-party cookies (e.g., analytics providers, ad networks) or processors.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] Not addressed. Many cookie providers transfer data outside the EEA — this is not disclosed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PARTIAL] Not applicable at banner level, but DPO contact should be accessible from the banner via a privacy policy link.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] Not applicable at banner level.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 7 (invalid consent mechanism), Art. 5 (no specific purpose, no retention period), Art. 15-21 (no withdrawal or rights mechanism), Art. 28 (no third-party disclosure), Art. 46 (no transfer disclosure)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Replace implied consent with an explicit opt-in mechanism (e.g., Accept / Reject buttons with no pre-selection)
- Categorise cookies by type (necessary, analytics, marketing) with individual toggle controls
- State retention period for each cookie category
- Provide a clear link to withdraw consent at any time
- Disclose all third-party cookie providers and link to their privacy policies
- Address international data transfers and applicable safeguards
